title: selinum ostruthium
intro: Selinum ostruthium, also known as Wild Celery or Wild Lovage, is a perennial plant in the carrot family native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, primarily for its carminative and diuretic properties, which help to alleviate digestive issues and relieve urinary tract problems. The medicinal properties of Wild Celery include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural diuretic, making it beneficial for treating conditions such as bloating, indigestion, and urinary tract infections. Wild Celery can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or dried root, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
